# SISMID `R Programming for Infectious Disease Modeling` Materials (2026)

This is the GitHub repository hosting the code and resources for the
SISMID 2026 R Programming for Infectious Disease Modeling module.

## About This Course

### Module Summary

This module builds practical R programming skills for infectious disease
data analysis and modeling. The 2026 course moves from reproducible
Quarto reports to statistical work in R, iteration, functions,
functional programming, formulas and model objects, ordinary
differential equations, and disease mapping/GIS.

Hands-on use of R is a major component of this module. Participants
should bring a laptop and expect to use R throughout the course.
Examples and exercises use data drawn from biological, medical, and
infectious disease applications.

Optional, advanced, or take-home materials are available for
bootstrapping, Arrow/parquet workflows, power simulation, maximum
likelihood with `optim()`, and ODE parameter fitting, but those topics
are not part of the required 2026 core schedule.

### Prerequisites

Participants are expected to have a basic understanding of R, including
how to open and write R scripts, read in data, and use basic functions
and syntax. Participants are also expected to have prior knowledge of
basic descriptive statistics and regression modeling consistent with an
introductory statistical course.

**Before class starts, you should install R (4.4.1 or newer), RStudio,
and Quarto (1.6.0 or newer) on your computer.**

### Core Module Content

- Create and render Quarto documents
- Use R for statistical summaries, tests, and model interpretation
- Write and use control flow tools, including loops and if/else
  statements
- Write, use, and debug custom functions
- Use functional programming tools such as the base R apply family
- Use R formulas, model objects, and S3 methods in common analysis
  workflows
- Solve basic ordinary differential equations in R
- Use GIS and disease mapping tools in an infectious disease context

### Optional / Take-Home Content

- Bootstrapping for confidence intervals
- Arrow and parquet workflows for large data
- Power simulation
- Maximum likelihood with `optim()`
- ODE parameter fitting with `deSolve` and `optim()`

General sample-size package demonstrations, the advanced linear models
draft, and the old combined ODE/optimization deck are archived for 2026
and are not part of the learner-facing core course.

## Repository File Structure

- `README.qmd`: controls what shows up here on the GitHub page.
- `index.qmd`: this is the website homepage.
- `schedule.qmd`: info on the course schedule and logistics.
- `references.qmd`: course references and external resources.
- `data`: datasets used in the course and exercises.
- `exercises`: student-facing exercises.
- `format`: website styling.
- `images`: static image files.
- `modules`: learner-facing core and optional module source files.
- `archive/2026`: material preserved from earlier planning but not
  presented as the 2026 learner-facing course.
- Everything else is housekeeping files needed for the website.

## Built With

- [Quarto](https://quarto.org)

## Contributing

If you see any issues, please open an Issue or Discussion on the book’s
GitHub page. Or, if you know Git, you can open a Pull Request to fix the
issue directly, which is even better!
